---
title: "Beam and Distributions"
description: "Define a beam, particle distribution, emission sources, and source lists."
audience: user
status: experimental
last-reviewed: "2026-07-22"
---

An OPALX run separates the physical beam from the rule used to generate
particles:

1. `DISTRIBUTION` defines the phase-space sample.
2. `EMISSIONSOURCE` associates emission behavior with a distribution.
3. `EMISSIONSOURCELIST` groups one or more sources.
4. `BEAM` specifies the species, reference momentum, charge, allocation, and
   source list.

This separation permits several source definitions to feed the same tracked
beam. Distribution attributes and accepted species are undergoing a
source-level audit; until that table is complete, use current regression inputs
and confirm the selected attributes in the generated API reference.

## Reproducibility

Record the OPALX revision, distribution seed, macro-particle count, bunch
charge, and all referenced external files with each production result. Large
reference distributions and datasets belong in the separate OPALX documents
repository rather than this text manual.
